name: The Anchorcraft
title: Chapter VII ‚Äî The Anchorcraft
objective: Fell the ice-locked coil mounts, forge the burn-seal, settle the helm rite, and reach the helm ‚Äî launch the frozen Anchorcraft before the glaciers close

npcs.name: The Pilot‚Äôs Plaque
lines: IN MEMORIAM ‚Äî she flew between checkpoints, and rests frozen between them too.
The rite, continued: first, fell the six mounts the ice has claimed. Then the seal that cannot be unsaid.
Glyph, then seal, then bulkhead, then helm. The launch is a promise read in order, and finality signs it.
SEALED ‚Äî READ AT INCLUSION. The hold will not catch until the helm rite settles.

npcs.name: Elder Sefa Tenwright
lines: Ten keepers founded this village. I have buried six in the snow. You see the arithmetic of my problem.
The craft in the ice fell before the Fall finished falling. Six coil-mounts froze solid ‚Äî fell them, every one, and the hull will breathe.
Hask keeps the burn-anvil aft. A proof fragment and the count of shards mint the seal that thaws the chamber.
The helm answers four runes in order ‚Äî the breath the field takes together, then one voice, once, and only once. The pilot‚Äôs plaque keeps the rest.
Throw the airlock bulkheads when the seal is forged. The Anchor takes no fractions, operator. Neither does the ice.
Reach the helm last of all. Behind you, a world that holds; ahead, one that doesn‚Äôt yet.

npcs.name: Mirelle, Corridor Warden
lines: You don‚Äôt move. The ice agrees you‚Äôve slid. Try to be the kind of person glaciers agree with.
The skitter nest at the north-west drift is cold made hungry. Thin it out and the road breathes again.
Watch the leads ‚Äî the cracks in the floe don‚Äôt lose travelers. They reconsider them, slowly, going down.
Take the skiff if a lead bars you. I never send anyone across mid-slide.

npcs.name: Hask Embervein, Forgekeeper
lines: One key is a confession. Seven keys are a country. Burn-seals are colder comfort, but they thaw a hull.
A keeper fell in the skitter nest, north-west, carrying a proof fragment. Bring me fragments, not promises ‚Äî the anvil cannot interpolate promises.
Six fragments make slag. Don‚Äôt look at me like that ‚Äî six is not a quorum at this anvil either.
Count your hammer blows out loud over the wind and you will never lose track of what is true.

quests.title: Fell the six ice-locked coil mounts
quests.hint: Six coil-mounts froze around the hull. Shatter every one ‚Äî while one stands, the burn chamber stays cold.

quests.title: Forge the burn-seal at Hask‚Äôs anvil
quests.hint: A proof fragment and twenty shards at the aft anvil. The anvil cannot interpolate promises.

quests.title: Settle the helm rite ‚Äî light the glyph set in order
quests.hint: It begins with the breath the field takes together, then one voice, once, and only once. The pilot‚Äôs plaque keeps the order.

quests.title: Throw the airlock bulkhead
quests.hint: Seal the airlock corridor ‚Äî one agreed state, and you are simply already inside.

quests.title: Reach the helm ‚Äî launch the Anchorcraft
quests.hint: Behind you, a world that holds. Ahead, one that doesn‚Äôt yet.

quests.title: Clear the north-west skitter nest
quests.hint: The nest at the north-west drift is cold made hungry. Ten skitters down and the road breathes again.

quests.title: Recover the fallen keeper‚Äôs proof fragment
quests.hint: Bring me fragments, not promises. The anvil cannot interpolate promises.

intro.title: Chapter VII ‚Äî The Anchorcraft
lines: Act II. East of the village, a starcraft lies where it fell ‚Äî hull frozen into the glacier, helm sealed under frost.
Elder Tenwright keeps the salvage ledger: six coil-mounts, one burn-seal, the helm rite in order.
The Crossing continues. This Anchor takes no fractions ‚Äî and neither does the ice.

outro.title: Relight
lines: The burn chamber catches with a chord, and the frost walks aft to fore in retreating sheets.
The field goes gold edge to edge across the floe ‚Äî the corridor of sky settles.
The Anchorcraft rises off the glacier on one long wave-tunnel.

outro.title: The Crossing Continues
lines: Behind you, a world that holds. Ahead, one that doesn‚Äôt yet.
Next: the Prover Array ‚Äî where mountains are ground into receipts.
